slides.title: Mass Producing Objects 🏭
slides.content:
Just like you can create an array of 100 `int`s, you can create an array of 100 completely custom structs.

This is how games handle massive amounts of enemies, bullets, or particles: by grouping them in structured arrays.

slides.title: The Struct Array
slides.content: We declare an array of `struct Enemy` just like any regular variable limit.

quizData.question: If you write `army[1].hp = 100;`, what exactly are you modifying?

options.text: The health of the second enemy in the array.
The memory size of the array.

quizData.explanation: You first pick out the specific struct using `army[1]`, and then you dive into its specific data using `.hp`.

slides.title: Initialization Shortcut ⚡
slides.content:
You don't have to assign every variable piece by piece.

You can initialize a struct instantly using curly braces `{}`, providing values in the exact same top-to-bottom order they were defined in the blueprint.

slides.title: Instant Initialization
slides.content: Watch how we quickly create 'Bob' with 50 HP.
